records.ety: [L. fabricatus, p. p. of fabricari, fabricare, to frame, build, forge, fr. fabrica. See Fabric, Farge.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fabricated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Fabricating (?).]

defs: To form into a whole by uniting its parts; to frame; to construct; to build; as, to fabricate a bridge or ship.
To form by art and labor; to manufacture; to produce; as, to fabricate woolens.
To invent and form; to forge; to devise falsely; as, to fabricate a lie or story.
quotes: Our books were not fabricated with an accomodation to prevailing usages.
